title: Well I Remember How You Smiled
content:
Well I remember how you smiled
To see me write your name upon
The soft sea-sand... "O! what a child!
You think you're writing upon stone!"

I have since written what no tide
Shall ever wash away, what men
Unborn shall read o'er ocean wide
And find Ianthe's name again.
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Well I Remember How You Smiled"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
